text: <par>Tagin <latinform>nyaruŋ a roŋ</latinform> implies that <latinform>ruŋ</latinform> HORN/EAR is distinct from <latinform>aroŋ</latinform> HOLE (cf. Jackson’s Mirish reconstruction for HOLE).</par>

The a pa in the second part of the compound is probably not #6090 *pa, but rather 'uncle', cf. the
Bengni form pa-tɯː a-puː.
<par>Looks like the vowels between the two morphemes may be switched? cf. Tagin DG do-ri in JS, HCST:144, though not sure if KDG-Tag was the source for JS's Tagin data.</par>
